content:
- SHORT-TERM TRADING
Welcome to March 2017 content for the ICT mentorship. This month we're teaching short-term trading. This
is lesson one: combining higher time frame monthly and weekly ranges.

section_title: ICT'S PREFERRED TRADING STYLE AND FUTURE CONTENT SCOPE
content:
Short-term trading is my cup of tea, my go-to discipline trading style. In this month, we're going to be
teaching in great detail my one shot one kill approach to trading, not just the Forex market but all the other
asset classes will be touched on in scope. As we get to those later discussions, in the middle of the
summer (June), we will be teaching bond trading, index trading, stock trading, and commodity trading. I'll
touch on this specific trading model in higher detail when I go into those respective discussions.

section_title: UTILIZING MONTHLY AND WEEKLY CHARTS FOR SETUP FRAMING
content:
We use both the monthly and weekly charts to frame the setups. We trade in the direction of the present or
next week's probable range.

section_title: WEEKLY RANGE AS THE BACKBONE FOR SHORT-TERM SUCCESS
content:
It's essential that you understand that the weekly range is going to be the backbone to your success in
short-term trading. I'm going to go through a lot of teaching and concepts as it relates to producing
consistent results when you're looking for forecasting the next week or current week's range.

A short-term model can be both trend or range-bound trading. Trades that are clear to see forming are the
goal.

section_title: IMPORTANCE OF CLEAR SETUPS AND PATIENCE
content:
Specifically with this trading model, try your best to keep from forcing yourself to take a trade if it's not
obvious. If it's simply just not clear enough on your charts, just simply sit on your hands and wait. These
types of setups form every single week. There's absolutely no reason for you to rush. If you do, believe me,
you will have psychological tug of war going on, you're going to have emotional issues, and the bottom line
is you're ultimately probably going to lose some money.

- HIGHEST PROBABILITY DISCIPLINE
Short-term trading, in my opinion, is the highest probability discipline. It offers frequent setups and with
consistency, it provides a plethora of trades.

section_title: COMPLEMENTARY NATURE WITH JANUARY AND FEBRUARY MENTORSHIP
content:
Before we get into this discussion about short-term trading, let me preface it by saying it's important you go
through your notes from the January long-term trading model and from February with the swing trading
model notes, because those notes will complement this specific whole entire month.

section_title: THE VALUE OF REPEATED REVIEW OF NOTES
content:
It'd be very easy for me to load you down with rehash of everything that was discussed in January and
February, but what I want to do is I want to keep things moving along. Number one, I want the lessons to be
fresh, and I also want you to be forced, if you will, to go back to your notes. It's simply me giving you the
PDF file is not enough; you need to be referring to them every single month. You get something new, when
you finish that new month, go back from the previous months and go through all those lessons again. And
yes, it takes a lot of time. That's why we're doing a 12-month mentorship. It takes a great deal of time to go
through this information, to digest it, to retain it, and then to be able to use it. It's simply not enough to give
me this and then I'll know what to do. It doesn't work like that. Obviously, some of you already realize that
and it's requiring you a great deal more work, but the ones that are doing it, guess what? You already see
the fruits of that.

section_title: UNREVEALED NUANCES OF ONE SHOT ONE KILL
content:
While many of the folks on the internet think they understand how I do one shot one kill, believe me, there's
a whole lot of things that I did not reveal in the first four lessons. This month will look somewhat like you
have already seen it, but there's subtle nuances, especially with one specific component that I'm going to
release at the very last slide in this presentation. I want you to be really focused on because it really is, in
my opinion, one of the telltale signs that you got a tiger by the tail. In other words, you have a big move still,
and I'll get to that when we get to the last slide in this presentation.

section_title: ICT'S PERSONAL AFFINITY FOR SHORT-TERM AND DAY TRADING
content:
I want you to know that when we go through short-term trading, this is my favorite form of trading. If I can't
find a setup for one shot one kill, the next thing I'm doing is day trading, and guess what? We teach that
next month. But this month and next, this is where I excel as a trader because of my personal unique
makeup as a trader and as a person. My psychology about things in nature is very short term. I can change
my mind very quick. I can make a decision rather quickly, so that means that I can do very well in this
specific discipline of trading and with intraday day trading.

While this may not be your particular style of trading, trust me, I'm going to teach you things in this month
and next month that will help you really, really excel as a long-term trader or a swing trader. Everything
blends together; that's why we have to do these things from a higher time frame down, and we go back to
the higher time frame and fill in gaps that were left there intentionally. Some of you have scratched your
head saying, 'Well, wait a minute, I feel like there's some things missing from the swing trading model.' The
things that are missing will be helped by filling in with the short term because everything is modular,
everything is complementary as we go through deeper study on this mentorship. So as we get down to the
smaller time frames, everything fills in, and then you'll go back in the higher time frames and say, 'Okay,
now I know what I'm doing, I know what I'm looking for, and everything fits together like a dovetail perfectly.'

- BACKBONE OF TRADE FRAMING
As a reminder, every discipline we will always be referring to the PD array Matrix. I want to not just go over
everything that you've seen here so far, and I'm not trying to belabor it, but it's important we understand
that as a short-term trader, especially for one shot one kill, which is like the flagship of this month's content.
That's what I'm that's my hallmark as a trader. I mean, that's what makes ICT, who he is: I can trade the
weekly range and get pretty close to what the high and low is for the week. You've already seen many
examples of me calling the intraday highs and lows before the fact within one or two pips, and sometimes
right on the pip.

section_title: INSIGHT INTO RISK REDUCTION FOR SWING AND LONG-TERM TRADERS
content:
Those same components are applicable to the weekly range, which if you really stop and think about that,
think about the Sunday open to Friday's close how many pips is offered over that time period? A lot, tons.
So if you can really focus in on what we're teaching this month, even if it's not going to be your discipline of
choice or style of trading, it will give you a greater insight on how you can reduce your risk trading as a
swing trader and or a long-term trader. As it relates to PD array Matrix, this is again the backbone to what
we look for and how we frame our trades. It ends up in every one of our teachings as we go forward in each
new discipline. This slide will always be there because I want to ingrain it in your brain, because this is what
I do from an institutional mindset. I look for how the institutions will move from a premium to a discount
array or from a discount array to a premium array. You have to understand that.
EXAMPLE
- BEARISH SCENARIO WITH FAIR VALUE GAP AND BULLISH ORDER BLOCK
Just an example, let's just take a look at one real quick. Let's say we looked at the market and it just
recently filled in a fair value Gap, and we think the market's bearish. Well, we go through this process of
looking for potential future discount PD arrays. We look and we can't see anything in terms of the bullish
mitigation block, we don't see any bullish Breakers, there's no liquidity void, but there is no fair value gap
either. All of a sudden it reduces us down to a bullish order block. So if we're looking at a monthly higher
time frame premium array, we're looking for an opposing weekly discount array. So in this case, we'd be
looking for the movement from a fair value Gap that has filled in at a premium level, we're dropping down
and looking for a weekly discount array, and if our analysis leads us to only a bullish order block below us in
the weekly format or time frame, that's the range that we're going to be looking for. That's your potential
range.

section_title: DEFINING THE RANGE FOR PROFITABILITY AND SCALING OUT
content:
That's how much you can frame your risk to reward model. That's the range you're trading. That's not to be
broken up and looked at as, 'Okay, well, if this range is 300 pips, I'm just going to take 20 pips and be done
with it.' No, you're going to look to frame your trades as you scale out. You're holding for these longer term
objectives throughout the week. You're going to be learning to take some profits along the way, but you're
holding out ultimately with that last portion of your trade to get down to that bullish order block. This is just
one example. It could be any combination of how a premium moved to a discount array or a discount array
moves up to a premium in terms of reaching for how the market may reach in terms of moving higher or
lower.

- MONTHLY TO OPPOSING WEEKLY PD ARRAY
I'll say this in simple terms: all we're doing is looking for a monthly PD array, whether it's discount or
premium, based on our next logical price move. Has it gone most likely higher or lower from where we're
right now? We move from a monthly PD array. Now, if it's bullish, obviously we're going to be focusing on a
monthly discount array. If it's bearish, we're looking for a premium monthly array. From that monthly array,
you're going to look for an opposing weekly array. So if we're starting from a premium on a monthly basis,
you're going to be looking for a weekly discount. That's your target. You're looking for that range to close in
or the market to be drawn to that level.

section_title: MARKET CONFIDENCE AND IDENTIFYING ACTIVE PD ARRAYS
content:
Now, if you're not really paying attention, if that didn't hit you hard, believe me, when you go through all
your charts, go back and look at every major move. That's what's been going on. That's what makes my
ability to call the markets from one level to the next and be pretty solid in terms of why I think the market's
going to go one direction over the other. When I'm not confident about that, it's because I don't I don't
know which PD array is going to be used next, so I'm waiting for more information, or the PD arrays have
been expired, they've already been traded to, so you have to look for something new, and that means you
have to wait. But guess what that does? It gives you context and also gives you patience. It gives you the
ability to wait. Now you know what you're waiting for because right now I can't do anything.

section_title: ELIMINATION PROCESS FOR ACTIVE PD ARRAYS
content:
So when we go through this process of eliminating or what's available in terms of active PD arrays, we start
from a monthly. Where what is the monthly PD array that we're most actively traded from? Is it coming off
of a bearish order block? Okay, great. So what's the weekly discount array it's going to be reaching for?
There may not be a mitigation block, there may not be a bullish breaker, there may not be a void or a gap,
there may not even be a bullish order block that you could frame around with confidence. It may be a series
of all down candles. So what does that mean? You look for a rejection block or an old low, an old high.

Another example would be if we were looking from a discount monthly array up into a weekly premium
array. We could be looking for the market moving from a bullish breaker, for instance, and we could
potentially look for the market to possibly run to an old high or an old low. Again, that would be your
potential range. This is the maximum you're leaving your portion of your trade on to reach for it. It may not
get to these objectives, but if you leave a little piece on, many times you're going to find that if you do this,
you can actually make more than the trade if you had on the full position and left it, you know, to your first
objective and just simply took it all off there. That small little portion, if you leave it on and you hold it for
your maximum potential, that little portion many times will be what you would be making entirely if you just
took first profit at a smaller lesser objective.

- SHORTING OPPORTUNITIES
Okay, higher time frame sequence. We've seen this slide before, but now we're making one more addition
and we're making some additional notes as well. When we're looking for shorting opportunities, we're
moving from a monthly chart sell program. It means we're looking for the market to move away from a
premium array, and we're moving down into the lower time frame, which will be the weekly chart, and we're
looking for it to become a sell program as well. So we're looking for the market to move away from its
premium arrays down into a lower time frame daily or watching for price to move away from its individual
premium arrays down into a four hour chart looking for a sell program as well, moving away from its
premium arrays down into a one hour chart where we're again looking for the market to move away from
their premium arrays.

So if we look at the slide here to the right, we have the monthly, the weekly, the daily, the four hour, and the
one hour. We're focusing on the monthly chart as we're looking for a premium array to trade off of. That's
going to be our point of origin. From the monthly, weekly, daily, and four hour, these time frames, you're
scanning, searching, and trying to determine all of the PD arrays, not just premium and not just discount.
We're looking for all of them from each time frame because all of them will give us potentially new setups,
short-term trading or one shot kill setups. And also, if we're looking for a move in this case, a selling
opportunity, and we're framing it off of a monthly PD array from a premium level, that means we're in
basically an overbought condition, and we're going to be watching and expecting the price to move lower to
trade down into a weekly discount.

section_title: IDENTIFYING DISCOUNT ARRAYS TO BREAK IN BEARISH TREND
content:
So if we're going to look at the monthly, the weekly, daily, and four hour on both premium and discount
basis, as we've already talked about in previous disciplines and teachings in December and January, when
we do this, if we're bearish, if we're looking for the monthly premium arrays to influence price to go lower,
why is it important to look at the monthly, weekly, daily, and four hour discount arrays? Because we're
looking for these arrays in the discount form to break. We want to see them break, and we want to see
them continuously keep breaking until we get down to our weekly discount array.

section_title: EXECUTION TIMEFRAME AND AIMING FOR WEEKLY DISCOUNT PD ARRAY
content:
So for framing a monthly premium PD array, we're expecting lower prices from a monthly level, and we're
trying to get down into a weekly discount. Now, it could be any one of the discount arrays, whichever one
appears on the chart or the market that you're trading. Not every pair or market has every single discount
array. There's only a few that can exist; very rarely have you ever seen more than three. So if you focus on
what you're looking for in terms of monthly down into a daily, it gives you a very clear-cut thing to look for.
There's not a plethora of things to choose from, or, 'Well, there's so many things that could be, Michael,
you know, which level is it?' No, it's very obvious. We're framing the market contextually and conceptually,
so it gives us a way to break down the marketplace and define where that market may most and obviously
likely reach to. So we're scouting for opposing PD arrays from the monthly down into a weekly discount. So
again, monthly premium level, and we're trying to look for an opposing weekly PD array in the form of a
discount. From that monthly premium array, we drop down into a weekly, daily, and four hour at every
premium array for each one of those time frames, and we're scanning for all of the premium arrays where
we can take short-term trades from as a short. Every one of those at the weekly, daily, and four hour, if we
find a level like that, we refine it all the way to the one hour chart. Our one hour chart is our executable time
frame for one shot one kills or short-term trading. Once you have that executed, what we're doing is we're
aiming for that weekly discount PD array. There's your cycle, that's your process, that's your whole outline,
if you will, what we do with the information we learned from January and February, and what we're learning
so far is we're taking all this conceptually and breaking it down into a user-friendly format.

section_title: DEALING WITH MISSED MONTHLY ENTRIES AND LEVERAGING LESSER TIMEFRAMES
content:
We want a monthly premium array and we start there. We're looking for an opposing weekly discount.
Whatever that range is, it may be hundreds of pips or it could be a thousand pips, I don't know, it's going to
be unique to each market and environment. But we may have missed the real opportunity to trade off of
that monthly premium. Maybe you just sat down and said, 'Oh, look at this, it's coming off of a monthly
bearish order block,' so it's already moved away from it. You can't really use that to trade off of; you just
know that it's moving lower. So what do you do next? You drop down to a weekly. What's the weekly
premium arrays? There may be one it trades up to later on, or a daily chart, it may trade up into a daily
premium array, or a four hour. One of those four time frames is going to give you a setup based on the fact
that the monthly premium is overbought, most likely are going to trade lower, and we're going to be looking
for a lesser time frame discount array. What's less than a monthly? A weekly. We execute regardless if it's
on a weekly, daily, or four hour premium level from a one hour chart. We get down to the discount on a
weekly that's our objective. Throughout the time from that execution, if it's a weekly or it's a monthly level,
we could potentially see multiple short-term trades form. If it's from a four hour basis, you won't see that
many charge setups form, but nonetheless, there's still going to be high probability.

- BUYING OPPORTUNITIES
Okay, for buying opportunities, the same thing is repeated here, but in opposite. We're going to be looking
for a monthly chart buy program, so we're going to be looking at a monthly discount array, and we're going
to be looking for it to go into a buy program, in which we're expecting higher prices. Weekly chart, we drop
down into it and we start looking for all the discount PD arrays as well. And we look for the daily chart to
also move away from its discount arrays, and then dropping them to a lower four hour and then alternating
down to a one hour chart looking at all of the discount PD arrays to offer support and price. But ultimately,
we're framing it off of whatever the monthly chart discount array is that's the beginning basis point. So
we're starting from a monthly level. We're finding a discount if we're oversold or for any really deep discount
market. In other words, it's traded for a long time lower, and we found some old institutional price models
for bullish institutional order flow, for instance, a bullish order block, an old high or an old low, whatever has
caused the market to trade higher in the past all those are potential scenarios.

We're looking for a lower time frame opposing PD array. In this case, we're looking for a weekly premium
array. We're scouting opposing PD arrays, but we're starting from that monthly basis for a discount. So the
lesser time frame from a monthly is a weekly, and the opposing from a discount is premium. So we're
looking for an oversold condition on a monthly to a higher time frame premium array that would be seen on
a weekly chart. So that would give us a relatively intermediate term overbought scenario. It helps us frame
the model without using any indicators.

section_title: UTILIZING LESSER TIMEFRAME DISCOUNT ARRAYS FOR ENTRIES
content:
Once we arrive at discount monthly PD arrays, we go down through the monthly, the weekly, the daily, and
the four hour to reference all of its individual unique discount arrays. Every single one of these, even if we
missed the monthly discount array and price has already moved away from it, we can't use it, but we can
refer to it as an indication that markets wanting to go higher. So therefore, we're identifying that weekly
premium level as a target. So whatever that range is, that's what we're waiting for it to trade up to. Between
them two points, there may be thousands or hundreds of pips in terms of the difference between the
monthly discount level and the weekly premium. So we drop down to the weekly, the daily, and the four
hour for all of the discount PD arrays, but which order blocks, bullish Breakers, bullish mitigation blocks,
liquidity voids below current market action, fair value gaps below current market action, rejection blocks, old
candle bodies with long wicks below it? Maybe we want to reach down below that, or it could be just a run
on an old low or return back to an old high historically. Those are all discount arrays. We look for them from
the monthly and then drop down to a weekly, find whatever they are there on that time frame, then the daily
and the four hour. Any one of those time frames, we're searching and determining whatever the PD arrays
are from the discount basis, but we're framing the directional move from the monthly.

section_title: EXECUTION TIMEFRAME AND MULTIPLE SHORT-TERM TRADES FOR LONGS
content:
We time all of those potential setups with the one hour chart, aiming for that weekly premium array. And
again, it could be multiple short-term trades forming between the monthly, the weekly, and daily, getting to
that weekly premium array. Again, if we drop down to a four hour chart, once we get into the four hour, the
trades start to fall off a lot in terms of the frequency or multitude.

section_title: WEEKLY TO DAILY SEQUENCE FOR ONE SHOT ONE KILL
content:
- SHORTING
Okay, if we look at how we can use the same model dropping down from a weekly chart, we can start from
a weekly premium looking into a lower time frame daily discount. And we're going to be scouting opposing
PD arrays respectively. And from a weekly to daily to a four hour, we're looking for all the premium arrays
that we could short from, and we key off of whatever they are. We wait for price to trade to them and then
we use the one hour chart to sell short from that's our executable time frame, aiming for that lower level
time frame daily discount. This, my friends, is the one shot one kill setup. This is my bread and butter. This
is the one that has consistent setups every single week. There's something to trade off of, but you have to
go through the process of waiting for it to give it to you.

section_title: WEEKLY TO DAILY SEQUENCE FOR ONE SHOT ONE KILL
content:
- LONGS
The opposite is same here when we're looking for a weekly discount, scouting an opposing PD array for
daily premium level. We drop down from the weekly, the daily, and the four hour looking for all the discount
PD arrays, executing on any one of them from a one hour chart. And again, this is the one shot one kill
setup for longs.

- FRACTAL NATURE OF PRICE
Okay, we talked about the swing trade progression, and it's important to bring this back up again because
without this understanding, it's going to be a little too vague. But I gave you a model at which we thought
that maybe this is a six month outlook on price. Okay, maybe it's a little aggressive to say six months, but
just for the sake of argument and discussion, let's just say this whole entire range of higher highs and
higher lows is six months and we had the benefit of forecasting the potential move to see this unfold. Each
one of these price legs has an impulse leg and it has a retracement, then it's an expansion swing. Inside of
each individual impulse swing, there could be smaller impulse and expansion swings. Each expansion
swing, again, just like we saw in the impulse swing, could be broken down to a smaller time frame seeing
its own individual unique impulse swing and expansion swing. And this is repeated throughout the entire
fractal that is seen in price.

section_title: IDENTIFYING MULTIPLE SHORT-TERM SETUPS WITHIN FRACTALS
content:
As a short-term trader, with this alone, there is a plethora of many, many setups and signals that you can
find just trading with one market, one pair. If this is a whole landscape, if you will, of six months of price
data, this could be every low that it trades from and trades higher. Every single time it makes a low and
moves up graphically, that is a potential short-term trade. So just from this chart alone, if you take a
moment and count every time it creates a move up, use that lowest point at which you could frame a short
- term trade, count them, I'll give you a moment to do so. There's potentially just on longs, there's eight short
- term setups here just by breaking the fractal down one scale, but we can go further than that and break
down the smaller swings into their individual impulse swing, retracement and expansion swing. And its
smaller expansion swing can be further broken down. Each individual fractal can be broken down to a
lesser fractal. Now, if we count how many opportunities you could be a buyer when you're bullish and you
can frame the marketplace correctly, how many opportunities do you see here in terms of potential buys?
There's potentially 16 buying opportunities in this overall fractal. That's not counting any short-term trades
that you could have done on all the retracements. Every little red section in here, they could also be short
- term trades as well. If we couple those with the buying opportunities, there is a great deal more trades that
are available as a short-term trader.

- BULLISH MARKETS
Okay, the general concept Revisited: we're looking for a market that is poised to trade higher on a higher
time frame. Now we're going to be looking for seasonal tendencies to help us. We don't require it, but
they're always going to be a bonus. We're looking for interest rate driven conditions. In other words, is the
bond market moving? Is it stagnant? Is it going sideways? Preferably, we're looking for a market that's
moving around. Does Commitment of Traders support the hedging program about the commercials? Are
they buying? Are they selling? In this case, if we're looking for a bull scenario, we're looking for
commercials to be lessening their short positions or aggressively adding or already net long. And we're
using inter-market analysis to support bullishness. That could be in the form of supporting with SMT
Divergence or correlated pair SMT.

We're looking for a market that rallies higher then retraces, and this is going to occur Monday through
Wednesday. So now we're getting more time specific, and then we look for the market to expand up into
higher highs. So before we do anything, we're going to already have our point of origin. In other words,
that's going to be the reference point from the monthly standpoint, and or dropping down to the weekly,
daily, and four hour, and we're going to know what PD array in the premium we're aiming for. So we see
that impulse swing. Monday, Tuesday, or Wednesday, we were looking for an up move to suggest it is, in
fact, buying. So that impulse swing is the telltale sign that there are smart money players in the
marketplace pushing price higher. That's what we're looking for. Underlying, we're expecting higher prices
anyway, but now we're seeing it.

section_title: FRAMING THE RETRACEMENT AND DISCOUNT PD ARRAYS
content:
We're waiting for the retracement. That retracement is going to come again between Monday, Tuesday, and
Wednesday. It could happen directly right off of Sunday's rally, and then Monday may be the retracement,
or it could just start right from Monday and have a very shallow retracement of one day. Between Monday
and and Wednesday, during that retracement, we're going to be looking for or anticipating the next move
higher, driving its move higher into that premium array that we're looking for. That expansion swing is where
we're looking to be profitable. Framing the retracement from the point of origin, that's where we're looking
for our executable time frame: hourly, four hour, daily, weekly, monthly discount PD array. We're looking for
all those potential levels as price starts to drop down. Now, it looks like graphically it's only on Wednesday,
but for the sake of discussion and keeping the slide clean, this retracement and the discount PD array
Matrix is applied through the entire range across the days of Monday, Tuesday, and Wednesday. So in
other words, what I'm saying is from Monday, Tuesday, and Wednesday, during any retracement lower,
we're looking for a monthly, weekly, daily, four hour discount array to trade off of, aiming for the weekly
premium array.

And it has to drop down into a Kill Zone. Now, what Kill Zones could you be trading? Obviously, you all
know that I like to trade the London open and I like to trade the New York open. If you're trading the Aussie,
Kiwi, or Japanese Yen, you want to be including the Asian session Kill Zone. Okay, so you can go into my
free tutorial stuff and see what that actually is, but for a quick down and dirty time frame, what you're
looking for is 23:00 hours to well, basically, you're going to be looking at 6 p.m. to 9 p.m. New York
Standard Time. Whatever time is in New York, 6 p.m. to 9 p.m. There's your sweet spot for Asia. Okay, there
you go, nothing nothing easier than that now. And many times it can extend to 10 o'clock, but usually you
don't want to be doing anything after 10. The closer you get to 10, that's kind of like their lunch time, so you
don't want to do anything around 10. And ultimately, if price drops down, gives you retracement into a
discount PD array from a monthly, weekly, daily, or four hour level, you can see that also can be used from
an hourly executable time frame. So if you get an hourly discount PD array, they could be executed on as
well inside of a Kill Zone. Again, you're looking for that Monday, Tuesday, and Wednesday setup, but you're
aiming for the weekly PD array. If you don't watch online kill, you're looking for that daily PDA right but
framing off of a weekly discount.

- BEARISH MARKETS
Okay, the general concept Revisited again. In bearish markets, we're looking for a market that's poised to
trade lower on a higher time frame. We're looking for any market that would potentially move lower
seasonally, but it doesn't have to; we don't need to seasonal fancy there again. It's just always a bonus or a
plus, if you will, if we can use a market that has a bearish seasonal tendency. Our interest rates moving?
Are they stagnant? Preferably, we're looking for a market that has the bond market moving all around. The
tenure notes are moving around, that's held in consolidation. And Commitment to Traders, are we looking
at a lessening of long positions by the commercials, or are we seeing a heavily net short position being
built, or are they already net short? Those are the conditions we're looking for from a COT standpoint. And
inter-market analysis supports the bearishness. That means SMT Divergence is indicating weakness or
correlated pair SMT suggests that there should be an overall sympathy move lower for foreign currencies,
or for trading stocks you'll learn all that as well with the indices versus the leadership issues.

And we're looking for a market decline lower then retrace higher during Monday into Wednesday, and then
we're going to be looking for a market to expand down to lower lows. So we have our point of origin from a
higher time frame monthly premium array. It trades lower impulse swing, and then there's a retracement.
Again, this is all occurring between Monday and Tuesday and Wednesday. Any one of those three days this
scenario or condition has to be here. That retracement has to come up to a level of premium, and then the
expansion swing is what we're looking for to be profitable in, aiming for the discount array.

section_title: FRAMING THE RETRACEMENT AND PREMIUM PD ARRAYS (BEARISH)
content:
We're framing our trade with premium array Matrix. So we're looking for a monthly, weekly, daily, four hour
and one hour premium array to short from in a Kill Zone for time the analysis.

- DEFINITION AND ANALYSIS
Okay, monthly ranges. Our price is fractal, and higher time frame analysis benefits us as a result. We note
the high and the low of every month candle. We note the present range in terms of premium and or
discount. We consider where price on the monthly will or could draw to. In other words, what's the most
likely direction? There is generally four weekly candles in every monthly candle. Every week we study
where the monthly will likely trade next. Again, all we're doing is looking for probabilities. There's nothing
that I teach with 100 percent iron clad, 'this is the way it's going to happen every single time.' There's no
absolutions; there's only probability. So we're looking from these higher time frames to look for where is it
most likely reaching for it, because if we're trading off of a monthly chart and we can pick the most
probable direction, chances are even if we're wrong, you know, the lower time frames that we're going to be
executing on still might give us opportunity to be paid. And in usually monthly ranges, we frame trades on
the weekly range.

- DEFINITION AND ANALYSIS
All right, so what's a weekly range? Again, price is fractal, and monthly analysis builds on the weekly. We
note the high and the low of every weekly candle. We note the present range in terms of premium and or
discount. We consider where price on the weekly will or could draw to. Again, what's the most probable
direction in terms of where the weekly chart is trading and where it may range up or below or two? There's
generally five daily candles in every weekly candle. And every week we study how the weekly trades inside
of its monthly range, using weekly ranges we frame trades on the daily chart. We're watching to see if the
weeklies continuously expand and make the larger monthly candle move in the direction we thought they
were going to go. So each week we're looking for confluence and confirmation that will be anticipated
seeing on the monthly is in fact unfolding, because the weeklies will be pushing that direction hopefully in
our favor and then making that weekly range expand every higher or every lower weekly candle. We'll build
that bigger monthly candle in respective direction.
EXAMPLE
- JAPANESE YEN MONTHLY CHART ANALYSIS
Okay, so let's take a look at an example. All right, so here we're looking at the Japanese Yen, this is a
monthly chart. You can see from 2015 springtime going into June 2015, we saw the Japanese Yen, or
Dollar Yen pair, trade up into around the 125.80 level or thereabouts. And we moved from a low formed
back in 2013, and then we dropped from the 125s into around June of 2016. If you look at the total range,
we have obviously moved into a discount market. And we look at the market's old support levels found at
the February 2014 to June 2014 there was a lot of support seen in there. So price came all the way down
into the point of origin where we think the market will and with the benefit of hindsight here, I'm not going
to be slicing and dicing charts because we called the Japanese Yen lower, which is Dollar Yen higher, all
through the months in 2016 as we see here. But looking at this example, we have a point of origin where
we think price may want to go higher, and once we see that, we start looking at all of the monthly discount
PD arrays. I'm framing the last down candle right before the big move starts to take place. That is a monthly
candle, and we start looking at the monthly range. But that's not the monthly range we're trading in. That's
just a monthly range, so don't be confused when I start talking about monthly ranges. I may
interchangeably refer to a monthly candle where it's individual or independent range that's not the
monthly range that we're going to be referring to. I'm actually going to distinguish the two. Okay, so we have
that one month's candle range defined.

We're going to go back to on the monthly level, where are our monthly premium arrays? Well, you have a
breaker here. So we frame that, so there's a move that we can look up to for a monthly level. So we have
both our monthly discount and premium arrays. This is our monthly range. This is our range for trading the
monthly range. In other words, what's our probable range of probability or profitability? Where would we
expect to see moves take place?

We take that range and we apply it to a weekly chart and refine that down into this individual weekly PD
arrays in terms of a premium array. So we have here weekly premium arrays outlined. I didn't put every
single one of them on here just to keep the chart clean, so that way we can go through and have many

individual weekly chart and monthly chart, and you can see how price had respected a lot of the other ones
too that aren't being used here. If I just gave you the obvious ones, I use rejection blocks here for weekly
and premium arrays. So when price broke away from that 104.35 level and started expanding, reaching up

section_title: FOUR-HOUR CHART WITH DAY OF WEEK FILTER AND ORDER BLOCKS
content:
Now we got into a four hour chart, we can start applying all of the order block theory and rejection block
theory. And we're also incorporating now the day of week filter, which is Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday. So
I've shaded where every Monday, Tuesday, and Wednesday's range is, and I'm also highlighting order
blocks for bullish buy, and I'm also highlighting mitigation and also highlighting rejection blocks. All of these
are discount arrays. And the red shaded area up is a 118.70 level or thereabouts. That's the premium array
on a weekly basis that we're reaching for. So how many buying opportunities did you see here? Six. One
each week. Let's break it down a little bit more detail. Again, taking all the unnecessary things off the chart,
and you can see how price has moved rather uniformly and systematically each new week, giving you
buying opportunities.

And I will add all the individual day dividers, and I want you to take a look at something. As the weekly
ranges defined by the double line vertical dashed lines, okay, that delineates the Sundays. So everything to
the right of those double vertical lines that are dashed that represents a Sunday's trading. To the right of it,
you have Monday, Tuesday, and Wednesday shaded. Notice how the lows of the week are generally formed
in Monday, Tuesday, and Wednesday in this context.

Also notice that when you see the high that's formed between Monday and Wednesday, say for instance,
that there's a high that forms during the Monday through Wednesday range, when that high is taken out
intra week, this is significant because price will tend to expand aggressively towards the monthly and or
weekly premium array, and it's reversed if it's bearish. So what we're doing is we're looking for the Monday
through Wednesday range every single week. When that is bullish underlying in the market, and if you
break that range that's defined by technically Sunday's opening, but just for the sake of keeping things
germane and consistent, because I did mention how we didn't really refer to Sundays anymore. You can
use Sunday for completeness sake, but from a Monday's opening to Tuesday and Wednesday's range,
whatever the highest high is, when the markets are bullish, if that high is broken intra week, say for
instance on Thursday or uh you know, Friday, generally that is indicative of an aggressive confirmation that
we're going to be moving to our premium array that we were looking for or identified ahead of time. And
everything you see here would just be reversed for bearish markets. Monday through Wednesday's range,
if that is broken, when it's bullish, the high that's formed between Monday and Wednesday that is a
confirmation that you are in a buy program and the markets will expand aggressively to reach for your
higher level premium array. When the markets are bearish, the low that's formed between Monday through
Wednesday, if that is broken on an intra-week basis, that is confirmation that you are in an aggressive sell
- off or sell program, and your long-term higher time frame discount array that you're aiming for, it's a high
probability condition that's going to reach for that. It may not happen that week, but you're going to keep
looking for the next week to be bullish or bearish respectively based on that information.

So hopefully you found this teaching insightful. We're going to be building on a lot of these ideas in a lot
more detail leading into the ultimate delivery of one shot one kill in a process oriented rule-based
breakdown, what you do from the beginning to end to execution. And until next session, I wish good luck,
good trading.
